title: Rubric
description: Ground-truth grading rubric (spec §2.6, §5.5). Output of `rubric_from_memo` and `hypo_generation` (Path B). Grading is rubric-driven, deterministic given a rubric, auditable.

fact_summary.description: Short recap of the fact pattern — optional but useful for the grader.

required_issues.description: Issues the student MUST spot to earn full credit. Each item is weighted.

id.description: slug id, stable across rubric versions

weight.description: relative weight; sums to 1.0 across required_issues

source_memo_excerpt.description: verbatim memo text justifying this issue

required_rules.description: Rules the answer MUST state correctly — not the same as issues (issues are "this came up"; rules are "here's the doctrine").

tied_to_issues.description: issue ids this rule relates to

must_apply_to_facts.description: Pollack-style requirement that the rule be APPLIED, not just recited.

expected_counterarguments.description: Arguments in the alternative the student is expected to acknowledge (§5.5 Pollack's 'argue in the alternative').

anti_patterns.description: Question-specific pet peeves the grader must penalize (copied from the professor profile's pet_peeves plus any memo-specific additions).

prompt_role.description: The voice the answer must adopt — 'law clerk memo' vs 'client's lawyer' vs 'brief'. Wrong voice = lost points (§Appendix A).

sources.description: Artifact IDs (PastExam, GraderMemo) this rubric was derived from.
